items.title: CART split and recurse
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  rootNode["Root node: all training samples"]
  searchNode["Search all feature thresholds"]
  pickNode["Pick split with max impurity reduction"]
  leftNode["Left child L"]
  rightNode["Right child R"]
  stopNode["Stop if min samples / max depth / pure leaf"]
  recurseNode["Recurse on L and R"]
  rootNode --> searchNode
  searchNode --> pickNode
  pickNode --> leftNode
  pickNode --> rightNode
  leftNode --> stopNode
  rightNode --> stopNode
  stopNode --> recurseNode
items.caption: CART greedily splits each node by maximizing impurity reduction, then recurses until stopping criteria are met.

items.title: Classification prediction path
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  sampleNode["New sample x"]
  test1Node["feature j <= t?"]
  test2Node["feature k <= s?"]
  leafNode["Leaf: majority class"]
  sampleNode --> test1Node
  test1Node -->|yes| test2Node
  test1Node -->|no| leafNode
  test2Node --> leafNode
items.caption: Inference walks from root to leaf following axis-aligned threshold tests; the leaf stores the predicted class.

items.title: Impurity before and after split
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  parentNode["Parent impurity I(S)"]
  splitNode["Binary split on feature j"]
  leftImpNode["Weighted I(L)"]
  rightImpNode["Weighted I(R)"]
  gainNode["Gain = I(S) - weighted child impurity"]
  parentNode --> splitNode
  splitNode --> leftImpNode
  splitNode --> rightImpNode
  leftImpNode --> gainNode
  rightImpNode --> gainNode
items.caption: An informative split lowers weighted child impurity (Gini or entropy); CART selects the split with the largest gain.
